_meta.purpose: Last-words fragments for casualty tributes. Consumed by casualty_pipeline.gd. Bias-checked against docs/BIAS_GUARDRAILS.md.

die_in_throes: Tell them I kept the ledger. Tell them it balanced.
I was tired. That's all. Just tired.
The belt doesn't care. That's the point.
Don't write me into the tribute. I'm not worth the ink.
My second knew. She knew and she came anyway.
I told them the air was fine. I was wrong.
At least it was quick. At least it wasn't the cold.
They'll say I held the line. I didn't. I just didn't run.
The eng tech fixed it. I just held the light.
I thought I'd have more time. Everyone thinks that.
Don't blame the pilot. The pilot did everything right.
I saw the vein. I saw it and I didn't report it. That's on me.
My crew deserved a better captain. They got me.
The Trust owns the debt. I just paid it.
I wasn't brave. I was just the one closest to the door.
Tell my genship I didn't forget where I came from.
The math was wrong. I checked it twice. It was still wrong.
I'm not a hero. I'm just the one who didn't make it back.
The station was supposed to be empty. It wasn't.
I knew the risk. I took it anyway. That's not bravery — that's arithmetic.
My hands were steady. That's all I can say.
The AI asked me if I was ready. I lied.
I kept the ship together. That's enough.
Don't name anything after me. I don't want to be remembered.
The cold gets in through the seams. You can't seal it all.
I did my job. That's all anyone can do.
They'll find the log. They'll know what happened.
I wasn't scared. I was angry. There's a difference.
The belt took what it wanted. I just slowed it down.
Tell the next captain: check the seals twice.
I served T1 for six years. They didn't come. They never come.
T2 promised extraction. The promise was the product.
T3 scrubbers failed. We knew. They knew we knew.
T4 took my blood and called it research. I signed the form.
T5 settled my account before I was cold. That's efficiency.
T6 built the hull that killed me. They built it to spec.
T7 fuel burned clean. Everything else burned with it.
T8 wrote the story before I died. They always do.
The Trust doesn't mourn. It amortizes.
I was NAC-born. We don't cry. We file the report.
RRA taught me: the mission outlives the soldier. I believed it.
SAA means we carry each other. Carry me a little further.
ED procedure says: document everything. I documented. It didn't help.
B1 clearance codes don't work when you're bleeding out.
B2 salvage crews know: the belt gives nothing back.
B3 maintenance logs don't mention the ones who didn't make it.
The station architect built this room. I died in it. That's the design.
My genship sent me here. I don't blame them. Someone had to go.
The Trustee's program promised purpose. I found it. It cost everything.
I was a conscript before I was a captain. The belt doesn't distinguish.
The last thing I saw was the hex-map. It was beautiful.
I kept my head. That's all. I kept my head and it wasn't enough.
